subject: chemistry
title: 19M.1.HL.TZ2.33
question_html:
Methane reacts with chlorine in sunlight.

CH4 (g) + Cl2 (g) → CH3Cl (g) + HCl (g)

Which type of reaction occurs?

A. free-radical substitution

B. electrophilic substitution

C. nucleophilic substitution

D. electrophilic addition
examiners_html: 85 % of the candidates chose free-radical substitution as the type of reaction occurring between methane and chlorine in sunlight.
